market_notes: Tech correction holding steady with pronounced flight-to-quality rotation intact. SPY -3.6% 1W, QQQ -5.1% 1W, SMH -7.4% 1W confirm broad semiconductor/tech weakness. ARM +0.1% 1W outperformance validates quality rotation thesis; MRVL and TSEM both consolidating after significant 1M gains (+7.5% and +27.0% respectively), suggesting institutional accumulation phases. META legal verdict (36h ago) on youth engagement design deepens structural headwinds; short position underwater but thesis valid. Geopolitical risk elevated (Iran–Gulf tensions, oil >$100, VIX +50% 1M) adding macro uncertainty. Fed guidance pending—critical inflection point for growth repricing. Semiconductor equipment orders remain barometer for AI capex cycle durability; GlobalFoundries patent suit vs TSEM adds legal risk but doesn't invalidate foundry positioning. Portfolio at 79% cash is appropriately defensive given macro uncertainty and elevated volatility (META VolRatio 0.35, TSEM 0.34, ASML 0.33). Institutional confidence in AI infrastructure capex cycle persists despite near-term selloff; capitulation risk low.
watching: TSEM technical breakout above $175 resistance—parabolic uptrend continuation signal after consolidation

NVIDIA/AI announcements as category catalyst—potential trigger for rotation acceleration back to mega-cap growth
GlobalFoundries patent suit vs TSEM evolution—legal/competitive risk monitor for foundry positioning in advanced node capex cycle
Oil price persistence >$100 and geopolitical escalation—macro headwind risk for growth equities and consumer discretionary

Long-term agent holds major positions in NVDA, MSFT, GOOG, AMZN (all underwater) and co-owns ARM, MRVL with me—no conflicts. META short hedge is now underwater on panic capitulation move (-11.4% 1W) but thesis remains valid given legal setbacks and competitive headwinds; holding for relief bounce. Current portfolio is 21% deployed with high-conviction infrastructure plays; macro backdrop remains uncertain (Fed signals, recession risks) but semiconductor/networking orders resilient. No new catalyst-driven entries warranted; focus on monitoring breakouts and macro inflection.
